decisions.question: How should the functional test suite provide local git repositories so init/create/apply exercise real clone operations without a GitHub dependency?
decisions.outcome: Local bare repos (git init --bare) with file:// URLs. Source repos declared via [repos.<name>] with explicit url = "file:///..." fields (no [[sources]] org discovery). Workspace config repo is also a local bare repo. DeriveOverlayURL and OverlayDir are extended to handle file:// URLs so convention overlay discovery is testable end-to-end.

decisions.question: What Gherkin step vocabulary is needed to set up local git repos and assert filesystem outcomes in a readable way?
decisions.outcome: Introduce a localGitServer helper that creates a named bare repo and returns its file:// URL. Steps: 'a local git repo "name" exists' (bare repo, returns URL stored in state), 'a workspace config repo "name" exists with body: <TOML>' (bare repo containing workspace.toml), 'I run niwa init from "<url>"' (wraps init command), 'the instance "name" directory exists' and 'the instance "name" directory does not exist' (filesystem assertions).

decisions.question: Which scenarios should the first tranche cover to catch the known regressions and protect the critical path?
decisions.outcome: Four @critical scenarios: (1) happy-path init+create produces instance with cloned repos, (2) create on an existing instance produces a correctly-named -2 instance (regression for ConfigSourceURL lookup), (3) failed create due to missing required keys leaves no orphan directory, (4) init from a local config repo with a convention-discovered overlay includes overlay repos. A fifth scenario covers apply (re-running) updating managed files.
